text: Welcome, Zilaudio. Let's start with the first point on the agenda: the harvest.
Thank you, Karen. Sorry, could you repeat that? I didn't catch the last word.
Of course. The first point is the harvest, the soybeans from this season.
Now it's clear. Could you explain it a little more slowly, please?
No problem. During the meeting, we look at the price forecast for next month.
Sorry, I'm not sure I follow. What does forecast mean?
Good question. A forecast is what we think will happen, the price we expect in the future.
In other words, the future price. Thank you, Karen, now I follow.
Could you say it more slowly?

Good morning everyone, and thank you for joining today's quarterly meeting. Before we begin, a quick note on the agenda. We have three main points to cover: first, the harvest results from last season; second, the new logistics contract for grain transport; and third, the export price forecast for the next quarter. I know some of these topics are technical, so please stop me at any time if something is not clear. We will move fast, but there is no problem if you need me to repeat a point or explain a word. The meeting starts in five minutes, in room two. Please have your agenda ready.

Hi Zilaudio, this is Karen. I am calling about our meeting on Thursday. I want to make sure everything is clear for you before we start. If I speak too fast, please tell me, and I will slow down. And if you hear a word you do not know, just ask me what it means. That is completely normal, and it is a smart thing to do. Please read the agenda before the meeting, so you know the main points. See you on Thursday, and thank you.
Sorry, I'm not sure I follow.
Now it is clear, thank you.
I have a question about the price.
Please stay during the whole meeting.
Could you say it more slowly?
